person_name: Geta, Roman Emperor (r. 209–211), brother of Caracalla
person_name_full: Geta, Roman Emperor (r. 209–211), brother of Caracalla
notes: Roman emperor from 209 to 211 CE, brother of Caracalla, co-ruled with him before being murdered.

docs.title: Life of Caracalla
docs.author: Historia Augusta (anonymous)
docs.excerpt: A contemporary portrait, in the Stanza degli Imperatori in the Capitoline Museums in Rome, is identified as that of Caracalla. 4 After his father's death⁠ 8 he went to the Praetorian Camp⁠ 9 and complained there to the

docs.title: Life of Geta
docs.author: Historia Augusta (anonymous)
docs.excerpt: … by his brother with hatred, more affectionate than his brother toward their mother, speaking with a stammer though his voice was melodious. 2 He was very fond of bright clothing " so much so, in fact, that his father would laugh at him.
